description_long: This object is a belt-fed machine gun with a long, cylindrical barrel and an integrated bipod for stability during firing. The receiver and barrel assembly are primarily matte black metal, while the stock is made of wood with a thumbhole design, providing ergonomic support for the user. A green ammunition box is attached beneath the receiver, indicating a belt-fed system. The front of the gun features a fixed front post sight, while the rear has a simple notch sight. The bipod legs are extended, allowing the weapon to be deployed on flat surfaces. The overall construction suggests durability and functionality for sustained automatic fire in urban environments.
description: A belt-fed machine gun with a wooden thumbhole stock, matte black metal body, integrated bipod, and attached green ammunition box. Features fixed iron sights and a robust, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the barrel end, bipod legs, and the green ammunition box directly beneath the receiver.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the barrel, the wooden thumbhole stock, and the attached green ammunition box.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the stock, rear sight, and the alignment of the bipod and ammunition box.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the ejection port, bipod, and the detailed structure of the stock and receiver.
attribute_barrel_type: standard cylindrical barrel with integrated bipod attachment
attribute_magazine_type: belt-fed with attached green ammunition box
attribute_iron_sight_configuration: fixed rear notch with fixed front post
